title: A Leader Leaves the Justice Department
summary: President Trump has asked Attorney General Pam Bondi to leave her job after 14 months. Bondi was in charge of the Justice Department, which helps make sure laws are followed. She faced criticism for how she handled a case about Jeffrey Epstein, a man who did bad things. The president was also unhappy because she did not prosecute people he sees as enemies. Bondi said her time as Attorney General was a big honor. Now, people are wondering what will happen next at the Justice Department. Ali Rogin talked to Mary McCord to learn more about the future.

options: For handling the Jeffrey Epstein case
questions.correct_answer: For handling the Jeffrey Epstein case

questions.question: What did Bondi call her time as Attorney General?
Who discussed what's next for the Justice Department?

background_read: The Justice Department is a key part of the U.S. government that works to keep people safe and fair by enforcing laws. The Attorney General is its leader, appointed by the president. Sometimes, leaders leave their jobs, and this can lead to changes in how things are done. In this case, Pam Bondi's departure raises questions about future actions and priorities at the Justice Department.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Trump and Attorney General Pam Bondi
WHAT: Bondi is leaving her job after criticism and the president's frustration
WHERE: At the Justice Department in the U.S. government
WHY: Because of her handling of the Jeffrey Epstein case and lack of prosecutions of political enemies

perspectives.description: Some might see Bondi as a loyal ally who did her best in a tough job.
Others might think she did not do enough in important cases and should have been more independent.
